layman_headline: No clear swing trade setup today — the market is fearful and altcoins look weak.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is sitting in 'Fear' territory with a score of 26/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.2%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins and into Bitcoin. None of the altcoin candidates have a compelling combination of good chart structure, oversold conditions, and a news catalyst. Most are in downtrends with bearish chart patterns, and the few showing strength have already moved up significantly, making the risk/reward unfavorable for a new entry right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful but hasn't produced a high-quality oversold bounce setup yet. Better entries will come when we see a genuine capitulation flush or a clear catalyst that shifts sentiment.
expert_reasoning: Systematic review of all 10 candidates reveals no setup meeting swing trade quality criteria. HTX (chart bias +1.10, bullish marubozu) is the strongest chart but lacks volume data, funding rate info, and any news catalyst — plus RSI at 58 suggests limited upside. ZRO's RSI at 30.8 is approaching oversold territory (our most reliable technical signal at 75% win rate), but the bearish chart structure (-0.70 bias) and price below all MAs means we'd be catching a falling knife without confirmation. GRASS's -23% weekly drop is tempting for mean reversion but the bearish engulfing candle on a -0.90 chart bias screams continued selling pressure. BTC dominance at 56.2% is actively hostile to alt positions. The Fear & Greed index at 26 with an improving trend is worth monitoring — historically this has been a reliable signal (80% win rate) — but it needs to stabilize at extreme fear levels (<20) to signal true capitulation buying opportunities.
risk_factors: High BTC dominance (56.2%) continues to drain capital from altcoins, making alt swing trades inherently riskier
U.S. strikes on Iran introduce geopolitical tail risk that could trigger a broader risk-off move
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May suggests capital is leaving the crypto ecosystem entirely
watch_list: ZRO RSI dropping below 30 with a bullish reversal candle (hammer or morning star) would create a high-quality oversold bounce entry
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 ('Extreme Fear') that historically signals strong contrarian buy setups
BTC behavior at $64K — a decisive move above $66K could rotate capital back into alts, or a breakdown below $62K could trigger alt capitulation (creating better entries)
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear phase with BTC holding relatively steady near $64K while altcoins bleed. High BTC dominance at 56.2% and shrinking stablecoin supply suggest defensive positioning across the market, making this a poor environment for aggressive altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Market in fear mode with hostile alt conditions — capital preservation is the best trade right now.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with improving trend — not yet at extreme fear levels that historically produce the best contrarian entries (80% win rate from past trades).
ZRO RSI at 30.8 is the closest to oversold among candidates, but bearish chart structure (-0.70 bias) means no confirmation yet — our RSI signal works best with chart confirmation (75% win rate).
Stablecoin market cap shrinking $10B since May signals capital outflows from crypto, adding macro headwind to any long position.
U.S. launching fresh strikes on Iran creates geopolitical uncertainty that typically triggers risk-off behavior in crypto markets.
GRASS bearish engulfing after -23% weekly drop and HTX bullish marubozu with +1.10 chart bias were the strongest chart signals, but both lack confirming volume and catalysts.
